title: File Operations: Create, Copy, Move đź“„
description: Manage files and directories from the command line.

slides.title: Creating Files and Folders đź“‚
slides.content:
# Making Stuff

**`touch`** : Create an empty file or update the timestamp of an existing file.
```bash
touch mynotes.txt
```

**`mkdir`** : Make a directory (folder).
```bash
mkdir Projects
```

**`mkdir -p`** : Create parent directories as needed.
```bash
mkdir -p Projects/Hacking/Recon
# Creates all three if they don't exist.
```

slides.title: Reading Files đź“–
slides.content:
# Viewing Content

**`cat`** (Concatenate): Dump the entire file to the screen. Great for small files.
```bash
cat /etc/passwd
```

**`less`** : View a file page by page. Essential for large log files.
```bash
less /var/log/syslog
# Press Space to go down, b to go up, q to quit.
```

**`head` & `tail`** : View the first/last 10 lines of a file.
```bash
head -n 5 file.txt  # First 5 lines
tail -f log.txt     # Follow the file as it grows (Live monitoring!)
```

quizData.question: You want to monitor a web server's access log in real-time as new requests come in. Which command is best?
quizData.explanation: The `-f` flag for `tail` stands for 'follow'. It prints new lines as they are appended.

slides.title: Copying and Moving (cp & mv) đźšš
slides.content:
# Manipulating Files

**`cp`** : Copy a file or directory.
```bash
cp source.txt destination.txt

# Copy a directory and its contents (recursive)
cp -r /source/folder /destination/folder
```

**`mv`** : Move or rename a file.
```bash
mv oldname.txt newname.txt   # Rename
mv file.txt /tmp/            # Move to /tmp
```

**`rm`** : Remove (Delete). **WARNING: There is no Recycle Bin in the terminal!**
```bash
rm file.txt
rm -r folder_name            # Delete folder and all contents
rm -rf folder_name           # Force delete, never prompt. DANGER ZONE.
```

slides.title: The `rm -rf /` Meme đź’€
slides.content:
## NEVER RUN THIS

`sudo rm -rf / --no-preserve-root`

This command tells the system to **forcefully delete every single file on your hard drive**, starting from the root. It will brick your operating system instantly. 

Always double-check your paths before using `rm`.

slides.title: Finding Files: locate & find đź”Ť
slides.content:
# Where Did I Put That?

**`locate`** : Searches a database of all files. Super fast but may not be up-to-date.
```bash
locate nc.exe
# Update the database manually if needed: sudo updatedb
```

**`find`** : Searches the live file system. Slower but precise and powerful.
```bash
# Find all .txt files in /home
find /home -name "*.txt"

# Find files modified in the last 7 days
find /var/log -mtime -7

# Find files with SUID bit set (Security Auditing)
find / -perm -4000 2>/dev/null
```
*(Ignore `2>/dev/null` for now, it just hides error messages about permission denied.)*

slides.title: Finding SUID Binaries (Privilege Escalation) â¬†ď¸Ź
slides.content: This command is a critical part of Linux privilege escalation enumeration. It finds programs that run with the owner's permissions instead of the user's.
